nodalDepartmentName.label: Labour and Employment Department, Himachal Pradesh

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The "Paternity Benefit" scheme, introduced by the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HPBOCWWB), The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to registered workers and their families. Under this scheme, the wife of a registered worker is entitled to receive ₹6,000 per delivery, for up to two deliveries, as a form of maternal support. In addition, the scheme also offers ₹1,000 to the male beneficiary as paternity assistance on the birth of a child.
schemeContent.benefits_md:
1. The scheme gives ₹6,000 to the wife of a registered worker for up to two deliveries.
1. The male worker receives ₹1,000 as paternity assistance.
schemeContent.briefDescription: The "Paternity Benefit" scheme by the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board provides ₹6,000/- per delivery to the wife of a registered worker (for up to two deliveries) and ₹1,000/- to the male worker upon the birth of a child.

children.text: The "Paternity Benefit" scheme, introduced by the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (HPBOCWWB), The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to registered workers and their families. Under this scheme, the wife of a registered worker is entitled to receive ₹6,000 per delivery, for up to two deliveries, as a form of maternal support. In addition, the scheme also offers ₹1,000 to the male beneficiary as paternity assistance on the birth of a child.
The scheme gives ₹6,000 to the wife of a registered worker for up to two deliveries.
The male worker receives ₹1,000 as paternity assistance.
The Registration Process for Building and Other Construction Workers is as follows:
Step 1:
The interested applicant should visit the Labor Welfare Office (during office hours) and request a hard copy of the prescribed
application form
from the concerned authority.
Step 2:

In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste a passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).
Step 3:

Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with a wage slip or appointment letter from the employer, verifying 90 days of construction work. If unavailable, a certificate from a local authority (such as a councilor, executive officer, or Panchayat Secretary) can be submitted to verify employment.
Step 4:
After submitting the documents and payment, the worker will receive an acknowledgment receipt confirming their registration.
NOTE 1:
Ensure that the application is submitted within the prescribed period, if any.
NOTE 2:
After three years, the worker must visit the office to pay a renewal fee and submit proof of 90 days of work each year to maintain eligibility for benefits.
NOTE 3:
A one-time registration fee must be paid at the office, and it will be valid for three years.

applicationProcess.process_md:
**The Registration Process for Building and Other Construction Workers is as follows:**
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should visit the Labor Welfare Office (during office hours) and request a hard copy of the prescribed [application form](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/pdf/formno27_A1b.pdf) from the concerned authority. **Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste a passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required). **Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with a wage slip or appointment letter from the employer, verifying 90 days of construction work. If unavailable, a certificate from a local authority (such as a councilor, executive officer, or Panchayat Secretary) can be submitted to verify employment. **Step 4:** After submitting the documents and payment, the worker will receive an acknowledgment receipt confirming their registration.
**NOTE 1:** Ensure that the application is submitted within the prescribed period, if any. **NOTE 2:** After three years, the worker must visit the office to pay a renewal fee and submit proof of 90 days of work each year to maintain eligibility for benefits. **NOTE 3:** A one-time registration fee must be paid at the office, and it will be valid for three years.

children.text: Registration Process for Building and Other Construction Workers:
Step 1:
The applicant must visit the
official website
of the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
Step 2:
If you are a new user then click worker services , click on "
New Registration
Step 3:
Once the dashboard appears, locate and complete the form by entering all required details. Mandatory fields are marked with an asterisk (*), ensuring that all essential information is provided.
Step 4:
Attach all necessary documents, including ID proof and any other relevant certificates, as specified. Ensure the documents are clear and meet the format requirements.
Step 5:
After completing the form and uploading the documents, click on the "Submit" button. You will be directed to the payment gateway to pay the application fee online.
Step 6:
Upon successful payment and submission, a registration number will be generated. This number serves as confirmation of your application and can be used for future reference.
Scheme Application Process:
Step 1:
The applicant must visit the
official website
of the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
Step 2:
Click on the option "
Apply for Benefits.
Step 3:
Enter your beneficiary details and date of birth, then click "Search" to view your information.
Step 4:
Select the scheme you wish to apply for to avail of the benefits.
Step 5:
Enter all required information and upload the necessary documents as prompted.
Step 6:
Finally, click on "Submit Application" to complete the process.
Application Tracking:
Applicants can track their application status online through the
official website
of the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board. Relevant updates will be sent to the registered mobile number.

applicationProcess.process_md:
**Registration Process for Building and Other Construction Workers:** **Step 1:** The applicant must visit the [official website](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/index.aspx) of the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board. **Step 2:** If you are a new user then click worker services , click on "[New Registration](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/PublicPages/AddBeneficiary.aspx)." **Step 3:** Once the dashboard appears, locate and complete the form by entering all required details. Mandatory fields are marked with an asterisk (*), ensuring that all essential information is provided. **Step 4:** Attach all necessary documents, including ID proof and any other relevant certificates, as specified. Ensure the documents are clear and meet the format requirements. **Step 5:** After completing the form and uploading the documents, click on the "Submit" button. You will be directed to the payment gateway to pay the application fee online. **Step 6:** Upon successful payment and submission, a registration number will be generated. This number serves as confirmation of your application and can be used for future reference.
**Scheme Application Process:** **Step 1:** The applicant must visit the [official website](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/index.aspx) of the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board. **Step 2:** Click on the option "[Apply for Benefits.](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/PublicPages/ApplyForSchemes.aspx)" **Step 3:** Enter your beneficiary details and date of birth, then click "Search" to view your information. **Step 4:** Select the scheme you wish to apply for to avail of the benefits. **Step 5:** Enter all required information and upload the necessary documents as prompted. **Step 6:** Finally, click on "Submit Application" to complete the process.
**Application Tracking:** Applicants can track their application status online through the[ official website](https://bocw.hp.nic.in/index.aspx) of the Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board. Relevant updates will be sent to the registered mobile number.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md:
**For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:** 1. The applicant should be a resident of Himachal Pradesh 1. The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker.. 1. The applicant should be aged between 18 and 60 years. 1. The worker should have completed at least 90 days of building and other construction work in the last 12 months. 1. The registration of the applicant should have been renewed annually.
**For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:** 1. The beneficiary must be a Building and Other Construction Worker. 1. The worker should be registered with the "Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board". 1. The worker's membership with the Welfare Board must be active. 1. The wife of the worker should have delivered a baby.

children.text: For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:
The applicant should be a resident of Himachal Pradesh
The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker..
The applicant should be aged between 18 and 60 years.
The worker should have completed at least 90 days of building and other construction work in the last 12 months.
The registration of the applicant should have been renewed annually.
For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:
The beneficiary must be a Building and Other Construction Worker.
The worker should be registered with the "Himachal Pradesh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board".
The worker's membership with the Welfare Board must be active.
The wife of the worker should have delivered a baby.
